title: Chalk covers + dark-mode thumbnails
oneline: Chalk page thumbs + dark-mode picker swap work end-to-end; covers need 8 Gemini calls
summary: Extended gen-coloring-thumbs to also thumbnail every *.chalk.webp (94 files, offline, seconds) and added a theme-aware pageThumb() to books.ts plus a resolvedTheme() swap in ColoringBook.svelte — verified with real-app dark-mode screenshots (owl tile now previews the solid-sclera chalk it applies). No negation was needed anywhere: chalks are stored ink-on-white and the picker tile's existing --lineart-filter invert+screen renders the chalk thumb correctly, so the tile and canvas share one render path. Covers have no chalk assets (the gap), but gen-coloring-chalk already accepts explicit 'book/cover' args with gates intact, so cover chalks cost 8 Gemini calls plus a small Book.coverChalk wiring — no tooling changes.
tried: gen-coloring-thumbs.mjs extended to thumbnail .chalk.webp sources as {page}.chalk.thumb.webp (full-catalog run: 94 chalk thumbs, 0 Gemini calls)
books.ts: chalkThumbPath() + theme-aware pageThumb(); bookAssetPaths() lists chalk thumbs for check-assets/strip-native-assets
ColoringBook.svelte: page tiles + prefetch swap to pageThumb(page, orientation, resolvedTheme())
Before/after dark-mode screenshots of the live app (run-splotch driver + Playwright, splotch-theme=dark) for creatures, shapes, and the covers grid
svelte-check, extended books unit tests, and scripts/check-assets.mjs over the result
Assessed cover-chalk generation path in gen-coloring-chalk.mjs without burning API calls
worked: Chalk thumbs are a plain resize — chalks stay ink-on-white and the tile's existing --lineart-filter invert(1)+screen renders them as white chalk, identical to the canvas overlay path (the idea's 'negate appropriately' step is unnecessary)
Owl dark-mode tile now previews the actual chalk (solid white sclera, catchlights, wand star) instead of the hollow inverted pen; circle confirms the harmless minimal-delta case
check-assets validates all 568 assets including the 94 new chalk thumbs; regenerating existing pen thumbs is byte-identical, so full regen is safe
npm run check 0 errors; 9/9 books unit tests pass (tests extended for chalkThumbPath/pageThumb)
limitations: Cover half not delivered: no cover.chalk.webp exists for any of the 8 books, so book tiles keep the inverted-pen cover in dark mode

Generating cover chalks needs 8 Gemini calls (gen-coloring-chalk -- <book>/cover works as-is; eye gate auto-skips, no night-fill follow-up since covers have no fills) plus small Book.coverChalk + coverThumb() app wiring
+94 committed webp files (~600KB) added to web/static and native bundles
Two uncataloged stray assets on disk (shapes/heart-tall, objects/umbrella-wide) also get chalk thumbs — harmless but worth noting
